target.title: fix: recover crashed main sessions via lock-file detection fallback
target.body:
## Summary

After an unclean gateway crash, `cleanStaleLockFiles()` may find lock files from the previous process. When `cleaned.length > 0`, the existing `markRestartAbortedMainSessionsFromLocks()` recovers those sessions. However, the previous version of this PR tried to handle the `cleaned.length === 0` case by inferring crash residue from remaining locks using mtime-based heuristics, which ClawSweeper correctly identified as unsafe (false `abortedLastRun` risk for young unknown-owner locks, and missing recovery for live/reused-PID locks).

This version drives the crash fallback from an explicit **safe-to-recover** lock classification emitted by the cleanup owner (`cleanStaleLockFiles`), instead of having Gateway infer crash residue from all non-cleaned locks.

### What changed

- `src/agents/session-write-lock.ts`: `cleanStaleLockFiles()` now returns a `safeToRecover` array containing stale locks with a dead/absent owner (`pidAlive === false`) and no report-only stale reasons (`too-old`, `hold-exceeded`), excluding orphan-payload locks protected by the grace window (`lockInspectionNeedsMtimeStaleFallback`). These are locks that cleanup chose not to remove but are provably ownerless crash residue.
- `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.ts`: `markCrashedMainSessionsFromRemainingLocks()` now accepts `safeToRecoverLocks` instead of `remainingLocks` + `gatewayStartedAt`. The marker trusts the cleanup classification — no more mtime heuristics or `pidAlive` checks in the marker itself.
- `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts`: `cleanupStaleSessionLocks()` passes `result.safeToRecover` to the crash marker when non-empty. Removed `gatewayStartedAt` parameter (no longer needed).
- Tests: Updated all crash-fallback tests to use the new `safeToRecoverLocks` contract. Added regression coverage for young unknown-owner locks preserved by grace window, report-only stale reasons, and `removeStale: false` dead-PID classification.

### Safety

- The crash marker only acts on locks that `cleanStaleLockFiles` explicitly classified as safe to recover — no heuristic inference.
- Young unknown-owner locks (missing-pid/invalid-createdAt within the 30s grace window) are excluded from `safeToRecover` by the `lockInspectionNeedsMtimeStaleFallback` check, so they can never be falsely marked as crash residue.
- Live-owner locks (`pidAlive === true`) are never included in `safeToRecover` because they are not stale.
- Report-only stale locks (`too-old`, `hold-exceeded`) are excluded because cleanup deliberately does not remove them — the owner may still be working.

### ClawSweeper P1 resolution

| P1 finding | Resolution |
|---|---|
| Drive crash fallback from real cleanup classifications | Marker now consumes `safeToRecover` from `cleanStaleLockFiles()` instead of inferring from `remainingLocks` + mtime |
| Keep young unknown-owner locks out of recovery | `safeToRecover` excludes locks where `lockInspectionNeedsMtimeStaleFallback` returns true (grace-window-protected orphan locks) |
| Replace simulated cleanup proof with real cleanup | Proof below uses the actual `cleanStaleLockFiles()` return contract |

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior addressed**: Gateway crash → `cleanStaleLockFiles()` finds lock files but classifies none as stale-removable → `cleaned.length === 0` → `abortedLastRun` never set via cleaned-lock path. When `safeToRecover.length > 0`, the crash fallback marks matching running sessions for recovery.

**Real environment tested**: Linux (Ubuntu), Node.js 22.22, real OpenClaw checkout at `07 Open Source/02-projects/openclaw`, branch `fix/crash-session-recovery-80254`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts \
  src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts \
  --run --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
 src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts
  ✓ marks running sessions from safe-to-recover locks at post-crash startup
  ✓ skips sessions whose lock path does not match safe-to-recover locks
  ✓ trusts cleanup classification: live-owner locks never appear in safeToRecoverLocks
  ✓ marks sessions from safe-to-recover dead-PID locks
  ✓ skips locks with report-only stale reasons (too-old) not classified as safe-to-recover
  ✓ only marks crash-residue sessions when safe-to-recover excludes live-owner locks
  ✓ does not mark sessions when young unknown-owner lock is preserved by grace window

 src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts
  ✓ classifies dead-PID lock past grace window as cleaned (not safeToRecover)
  ✓ classifies orphan lock past grace window as cleaned
  ✓ does not classify young orphan locks as safeToRecover
  ✓ includes dead-PID lock in safeToRecover when removeStale is false

 src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts
  ✓ calls crash marker with safeToRecover locks when cleanup finds non-removable dead-owner locks

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  180 passed (180)
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- `cleanStaleLockFiles(removeStale: true)` with dead-PID lock → lock removed, enters `cleaned`, `safeToRecover` empty ✓
- `cleanStaleLockFiles(removeStale: true)` with young orphan lock in grace window → lock preserved, both `cleaned` and `safeToRecover` empty ✓
- `cleanStaleLockFiles(removeStale: false)` with dead-PID lock → lock preserved, enters `safeToRecover` ✓
- `markCrashedMainSessionsFromRemainingLocks` with `safeToRecoverLocks` → marks matching running sessions ✓
- `markCrashedMainSessionsFromRemainingLocks` with empty `safeToRecoverLocks` → no false marks ✓
- `cleanupStaleSessionLocks` passes `safeToRecover` to crash marker when non-empty ✓

**What was not tested**: Live Gateway crash/restart end-to-end on Windows. The `safeToRecover` classification contract is platform-independent because it derives from `cleanStaleLockFiles()` inspection logic (which handles OS-specific PID checks internally). The full Gateway crash → restart → session-restore flow was not tested in a live OpenClaw process.

Fixes #80254
target.author: tanshanshan
